reasoning: Pixel art heart heal VFX sprite shown at 8x zoom. Main structure is clean with hard-edge pixels and no anti-aliasing artifacts. Color palette is disciplined: black background, green outer border, yellow/gold inner border, white fill, accent colors (pink, yellow, light) for sparkle particles. No gradient shading — all hard tones. No sub-pixel bleeding or color bleed on primary outlines. Semantic read is correct (heart shape with sparkles = heal VFX). However: (1) Several isolated stray pixels appear near bottom-right of the large heart outside the main shape boundary; these may be intentional particles but distribution is irregular in a way that reads noisy rather than designed. (2) Sparkle dot placement inside the large heart is asymmetric left-vs-right without clear compositional intent. (3) Outline pixel thickness shows minor variation at a few corner transitions (1px vs 2px). None of these are decisive failures — the VFX particle intent makes many scattered pixels plausible — but the irregularities prevent a clean pass.
